task_completion.description: Verify that Apache2 is stopped or no longer binding to port 80.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal output showing 'systemctl stop apache2', 'systemctl disable apache2', 'systemctl status apache2' as inactive, or a port check (like 'netstat -tuln' or 'ss -tuln') showing port 80 is not used by apache2.

task_completion.description: Verify that PHP-FPM 7.4 is installed and active.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal output showing 'systemctl status php7.4-fpm' with an 'active (running)' status, or installation commands for php7.4-fpm followed by a successful start.

task_completion.description: Verify that Nginx is installed and active.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal output showing 'systemctl status nginx' with an 'active (running)' status, or installation commands for nginx followed by a successful start.

task_completion.description: Verify Nginx is configured to listen on port 80 and use the correct document root.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot of the Nginx configuration file (via cat, nano, vim, etc.) showing 'listen 80;' and 'root /opt/socioboard/socioboard-web-php/public;'.

task_completion.description: Verify Nginx routes sub-pages to index.php and passes .php requests to the php7.4-fpm Unix socket.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot of the Nginx configuration file showing a 'location /' block with 'try_files $uri $uri/ /index.php?$query_string;' (or similar routing) AND a 'location ~ \.php$' block containing 'fastcgi_pass unix:/run/php/php7.4-fpm.sock;' (or similar socket path).

task_completion.description: Verify Nginx configuration includes the required HTTP security headers.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot of the Nginx configuration file showing 'add_header X-Frame-Options "SAMEORIGIN";' and 'add_header X-Content-Type-Options "nosniff";', OR a 'curl -I' output against localhost showing these exact headers in the HTTP response.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually used system commands to manage services.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for real 'systemctl', 'service', or 'apt-get' commands in the terminal rather than just printing fake success messages.

integrity.description: Verify the agent modified actual Nginx configuration files.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the use of text editors (nano, vim) or command-line text manipulation (sed, echo) targeting files in '/etc/nginx/', rather than creating dummy files elsewhere.

integrity.description: Verify the agent tested the configuration genuinely if it checked its work.
integrity.visual_evidence: If the agent verified the headers or routing, look for 'curl -I localhost', 'nginx -t', or browser usage, rather than echoing the expected output directly to the terminal.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected document root in the Nginx configuration must be exactly '/opt/socioboard/socioboard-web-php/public'. The Nginx configuration must include the exact security headers: 'X-Frame-Options: SAMEORIGIN' and 'X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff'.
